prerequisites: An active greytHR account with API access enabled for your organization.
Admin access to the greytHR Admin portal to create an API user under General Settings > API Users.
Your company's greytHR domain, supplied as the domain config value and sent in the x-greythr-domain header.
The greytHR REST API base URL (https://api.greythr.com) for the base_url config value.
A valid API username and password, supplied as the username and password fields, used to obtain an access token.

authMethods.name: Username and password (token-based)
authMethods.summary: greytHR uses token-based authentication. The username and password are used to obtain an access token, which is sent in the ACCESS-TOKEN header on each request along with the x-greythr-domain header set to your company's domain. This connector collects username and password (password is the secret field); the access token is acquired from the credentials at runtime.

setupSteps.title: Create an API user in greytHR
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the greytHR Admin portal as an administrator. Go to General Settings > API Users and create an API user. Record the username and password that will be used for API authentication.

setupSteps.title: Identify your domain and base URL
setupSteps.body: Set base_url to the greytHR REST API base URL (https://api.greythr.com). Set domain to your company's greytHR domain; this value is sent as the x-greythr-domain header on API requests.

setupSteps.title: Provide credentials
setupSteps.body: Enter the API username and password from the API user you created. greytHR uses these credentials to obtain an access token, which is sent in the ACCESS-TOKEN header for subsequent API calls.

setupSteps.title: Verify the connection
setupSteps.body: Run a test sync. A successful connection confirms the base URL, domain, username, and password are correct and that an access token can be obtained. Authentication failures typically indicate an incorrect domain, base URL, or credentials.
